title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens ended his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
The Bruins anticipate signing Hagens to an entry-level contract, adding him for the final stretch of the season.
Boston GM Don Sweeney indicates Hagens' arrival depends on readiness to contribute in the playoff race.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Bradley Cooper directs Will Arnett in comedy-drama 'Is This Thing On?', now streaming on Disney+.
'Lone Wolf McQuade', starring late Chuck Norris, is available on Tubi, Pluto TV, Plex, Prime Video, and YouTube.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' features Cillian Murphy on Netflix, set in WWII Birmingham.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora discusses his Red Sox future, focusing on the current season and family considerations beyond 2027.
Cora's contract with the Red Sox runs through 2027, with potential extension discussions in 2026.
Cora needs 134 more wins to surpass Terry Francona's Red Sox managerial wins, likely achievable in two seasons.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line with Viktor Arvidsson, Pavel Zacha, and Casey Mittelstadt has outscored opponents 35-16 this season.
Boston's second line ranks second in NHL goal differential for forward lines, just behind Colorado's top line with a +19 differential.
Zacha leads the line with a career-best 23 goals, while Arvidsson and Mittelstadt have been key contributors this season.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support MGH Pediatric Cancer Center.
Mahr's inspiration comes from young patients he encountered during the 2014 marathon, highlighting the need for pediatric cancer research funding.
Mahr, running for the second time, aims to honor and support families facing cancer challenges at Massachusetts General Hospital.

title: ‘More money in my pocket is a no-brainer’: Readers weigh in on proposed income tax cut
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A proposed 1% income tax cut in Massachusetts could save median households $1,250 but cost the state $5.1 billion annually.
A Boston.com poll shows 78% support for the income tax cut, with 20% opposed and some residents expressing concern over state funding.
Governor Maura Healey opposes the tax cut, citing potential harm to state finances and economic competitiveness.

title: A voodoo doll, a love triangle, and an alleged poisoning: Testimony begins in Salisbury antifreeze murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judy Church is on trial in Salisbury for allegedly poisoning her boyfriend, Leroy Fowler, with antifreeze in 2022.
Prosecutors argue Church poisoned Fowler due to a love triangle, citing incriminating jokes and her possession of antifreeze.
The defense claims Church's statements were misinterpreted and that her filming was to highlight Fowler's substance issues.

title: Healey pushes to get rid of Sunday hunting ban
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor Maura Healey proposes lifting Massachusetts' Sunday hunting ban, allowing crossbow use and reducing bowhunting setbacks.
Massachusetts and Maine currently maintain a complete Sunday hunting ban; MassWildlife supports legislative changes for better wildlife management.
Proposed reforms aim to address deer overpopulation issues, supported by Mass Audubon and local conservation advocates.

title: Cape Cod magnet fisher pulls ‘pristine-looking’ derringer pistol from Bridgewater river
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nate DeMontigny found a pristine derringer pistol in a Bridgewater river, still in its original case.
DeMontigny's discovery, shared via his Cape Cod Magnet Crew YouTube channel, gained 1.2 million views on a subreddit.
After contacting police, DeMontigny reported the find, noting the gun's stainless steel helped prevent corrosion.

title: Teen charged with murder after stabbing in Chelsea
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old, Sergio Josue Castellanos, charged with murder after a Chelsea stabbing resulting in one death.
Chelsea police found multiple stabbing victims near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue Thursday at 6:45 p.m.
A 25-year-old man died after surgery; the suspect to be arraigned in Chelsea District Court Friday.
